articles.headline: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
articles.description: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

images.name: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
images.caption: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: England sign off WC prep with 3-0 win as Rice, Gordon, Watkins score
articles.description: Returning Declan Rice set England on course for a morale-boosting 3-0 victory against Costa Rica after a heavy thunderstorm in Orlando delayed their final World Cup warmup match by an hour.

images.name: Declan Rice [1296x729]
images.caption: Declan Rice opened the scoring for England shortly after 10 minutes played.

images.name: Tuchel: England can't be World Cup favourites
images.caption: Thomas Tuchel says England can't be favourites because there are "proven winners" at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
articles.description: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

images.name: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
images.caption: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

articles.headline: Argentina wins as Messi scores in cameo in front of 88k in Alabama
articles.description: Argentine legend Messi, who is working his way back from muscle fatigue ahead of the start of the FIFA World Cup, came on in the 70th minute and scored from the penalty spot right after in his team's 3-0 win over Iceland.

images.name: Messi celeb Arg Iceland_260609 [1296x729]
images.caption: Lionel Messi celebrates after scoring a goal for Argentina in a friendly against Iceland.
images.alt: Lionel Messi celebrates after scoring a goal for Argentina in a friendly against Iceland.

article.headline: Costa Rica beat Saudi Arabia amid protests in Newcastle
article.description: Costa Rica earned a 3-1 win over Saudi Arabia at St James' Park in a friendly on Friday after a pre-match protest by Newcastle United fans over the use of the stadium by the Saudi team.
article.linkText: Costa Rica beat Saudi Arabia amid protests in Newcastle

images.name: Keylor Navas Costa Rica vs. Saudi Arabia [1296x729]
images.caption: Keylor Navas of Costa Rica celebrates victory over Saudi Arabia in Newcastle.
images.alt: Keylor Navas of Costa Rica celebrates victory after the International Friendly match between Saudi Arabia and Costa Rica at St James' Park on September 08, 2023 in Newcastle upon Tyne, England.

article.story:
Costa Rica earned a 3-1 win over Saudi Arabia at St James' Park in a friendly on Friday after a pre-match protest by Newcastle United fans over the use of the stadium by the Saudi team.

The game also saw the return of Ticos captain Keylor Navas. The former Real Madrid goalkeeper had not played for Costa Rica since last year's World Cup in Qatar, missing a couple of friendlies and the Concacaf Gold Cup in July.

- Stream on ESPN+: LaLiga, Bundesliga, more (U.S.)

The Central Americans took the lead in the 12th minute when defender Francisco Calvo headed home Joel Campbell's cross.

Manfred Ugalde made it 2-0 just past the half-hour mark with another header in the six-yard box from Jimmy Marin's pass.

Saudi Arabia, who are now managed by former Italy coach Roberto Mancini, pulled one back in the 68th minute when Ali Al Bulayhi headed in Feras Al Brikan's pass from a corner.

However, they could not find the equaliser and Costa Rica wrapped up the win when Randall Leal fired home in the 89th.

"Great work by the whole team and good win, it's always nice to play again," said Navas on social media.

Costa Rica interim coach Claudio Vivas told ESPN: "Keylor Navas has a very distinguished career, playing for Real Madrid is important, it's not for just anyone. He's a great goalkeeper, he has a lot to give us, let's celebrate that we can have him,"

Costa Rica will next face the United Arab Emirates in a friendly in Croatia on Tuesday.

A group of Newcastle fans protested before the match against the use of St James' Park by Saudi Arabia, who will play again at the stadium on Tuesday versus South Korea.

Newcastle's majority shareholder is Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund and critics argue that the buyout of the club amounts to sportswashing by a regime keen to improve its image.

The two friendly games form part of the Saudis' preparations for the AFC Asian Cup to be held in Qatar in January 2024. Saudi Arabia won the last of their thee Asian Cup titles in 1996.

keyEvents.text: Goal!  Saudi Arabia 0, Costa Rica 1. Francisco Calvo (Costa Rica) header from very close range to the top right corner. Assisted by Joel Campbell with a cross   following a set piece situation.
Sebastián Acuña (Costa Rica) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.

Goal!  Saudi Arabia 0, Costa Rica 2. Manfred Ugalde (Costa Rica) header from the left side of the six yard box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Jimmy Marín with a cross   following a set piece situation.
Substitution, Costa Rica. Youstin Salas replaces Sebastián Acuña.
Second Half begins Saudi Arabia 0, Costa Rica 2.
Substitution, Saudi Arabia. Abdullah Al Hamdan replaces Abdulrahman Ghareeb.
Substitution, Costa Rica. Randall Leal replaces Jewison Bennette.
Goal!  Saudi Arabia 1, Costa Rica 2. Ali Al Bulayhi (Saudi Arabia) header from very close range to the centre of the goal. Assisted by Feras Al Brikan   following a corner.
Jefry Valverde (Costa Rica) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Yasir Al Shahrani (Saudi Arabia) is shown the yellow card.
Substitution, Saudi Arabia. Fahad Al Muwallad replaces Ali Abdullah Hazzazi.
Substitution, Costa Rica. Anthony Contreras replaces Manfred Ugalde.
Substitution, Costa Rica. Alexis Gamboa replaces Jimmy Marín.
Substitution, Costa Rica. Ian Lawrence replaces Joel Campbell.
Substitution, Costa Rica. Cristopher Núñez replaces Alejandro Bran.
Goal!  Saudi Arabia 1, Costa Rica 3. Randall Leal (Costa Rica) right footed shot from the right side of the box to the bottom left corner.
